inputs.name: create
inputs.description: Controls if resources should be created (affects nearly all resources)

inputs.name: tags
inputs.description: A map of tags to add to all resources

inputs.name: access_logs
inputs.description: Map containing access logging configuration for load balancer

inputs.name: connection_logs
inputs.description: Map containing access logging configuration for load balancer

inputs.name: customer_owned_ipv4_pool
inputs.description: The ID of the customer owned ipv4 pool to use for this load balancer

inputs.name: desync_mitigation_mode
inputs.description: Determines how the load balancer handles requests that might pose a security risk to an application due to HTTP desync. Valid values are `monitor`, `defensive` (default), `strictest`

inputs.name: dns_record_client_routing_policy
inputs.description: Indicates how traffic is distributed among the load balancer Availability Zones. Possible values are any_availability_zone (default), availability_zone_affinity, or partial_availability_zone_affinity. Only valid for network type load balancers.

inputs.name: drop_invalid_header_fields
inputs.description: Indicates whether HTTP headers with header fields that are not valid are removed by the load balancer (`true`) or routed to targets (`false`). The default is `true`. Elastic Load Balancing requires that message header names contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`

inputs.name: enable_cross_zone_load_balancing
inputs.description: If `true`, cross-zone load balancing of the load balancer will be enabled. For application load balancer this feature is always enabled (`true`) and cannot be disabled. Defaults to `true`

inputs.name: enable_deletion_protection
inputs.description: If `true`, deletion of the load balancer will be disabled via the AWS API. This will prevent Terraform from deleting the load balancer. Defaults to `true`

inputs.name: enable_http2
inputs.description: Indicates whether HTTP/2 is enabled in application load balancers. Defaults to `true`

inputs.name: enable_tls_version_and_cipher_suite_headers
inputs.description: Indicates whether the two headers (`x-amzn-tls-version` and `x-amzn-tls-cipher-suite`), which contain information about the negotiated TLS version and cipher suite, are added to the client request before sending it to the target. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Defaults to `false`

inputs.name: enable_waf_fail_open
inputs.description: Indicates whether to allow a WAF-enabled load balancer to route requests to targets if it is unable to forward the request to AWS WAF. Defaults to `false`

inputs.name: enable_xff_client_port
inputs.description: Indicates whether the X-Forwarded-For header should preserve the source port that the client used to connect to the load balancer in `application` load balancers. Defaults to `false`

inputs.name: idle_timeout
inputs.description: The time in seconds that the connection is allowed to be idle. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Default: `60`

inputs.name: internal
inputs.description: If true, the LB will be internal. Defaults to `false`

inputs.name: ip_address_type
inputs.description: The type of IP addresses used by the subnets for your load balancer. The possible values are `ipv4` and `dualstack`

inputs.name: load_balancer_type
inputs.description: The type of load balancer to create. Possible values are `application`, `gateway`, or `network`. The default value is `application`

inputs.name: enforce_security_group_inbound_rules_on_private_link_traffic
inputs.description: Indicates whether inbound security group rules are enforced for traffic originating from a PrivateLink. Only valid for Load Balancers of type network. The possible values are on and off.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the LB. This name must be unique within your AWS account, can have a maximum of 32 characters, must contain only alphanumeric characters or hyphens, and must not begin or end with a hyphen

inputs.name: name_prefix
inputs.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with `name`

inputs.name: preserve_host_header
inputs.description: Indicates whether the Application Load Balancer should preserve the Host header in the HTTP request and send it to the target without any change. Defaults to `false`

inputs.name: security_groups
inputs.description: A list of security group IDs to assign to the LB

inputs.name: subnet_mapping
inputs.description: A list of subnet mapping blocks describing subnets to attach to load balancer

inputs.name: subnets
inputs.description: A list of subnet IDs to attach to the LB. Subnets cannot be updated for Load Balancers of type `network`. Changing this value for load balancers of type `network` will force a recreation of the resource

inputs.name: xff_header_processing_mode
inputs.description: Determines how the load balancer modifies the X-Forwarded-For header in the HTTP request before sending the request to the target. The possible values are `append`, `preserve`, and `remove`. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. The default is `append`

inputs.name: timeouts

inputs.name: default_port
inputs.description: Default port used across the listener and target group

inputs.name: default_protocol
inputs.description: Default protocol used across the listener and target group

inputs.name: identifier
inputs.description: The name of the RDS instance

inputs.description: Determines whether to use `identifier` as is or create a unique identifier beginning with `identifier` as the specified prefix

inputs.description: RDS custom iam instance profile

inputs.name: allocated_storage
inputs.description: The allocated storage in gigabytes

inputs.name: storage_type
inputs.description: One of 'standard' (magnetic), 'gp2' (general purpose SSD), 'gp3' (new generation of general purpose SSD), or 'io1' (provisioned IOPS SSD). The default is 'io1' if iops is specified, 'gp2' if not. If you specify 'io1' or 'gp3' , you must also include a value for the 'iops' parameter

inputs.name: storage_throughput
inputs.description: Storage throughput value for the DB instance. See `notes` for limitations regarding this variable for `gp3`

inputs.name: storage_encrypted
inputs.description: Specifies whether the DB instance is encrypted

inputs.name: kms_key_id
inputs.description: The ARN for the KMS encryption key. If creating an encrypted replica, set this to the destination KMS ARN. If storage_encrypted is set to true and kms_key_id is not specified the default KMS key created in your account will be used. Be sure to use the full ARN, not a key alias.

inputs.name: replicate_source_db
inputs.description: Specifies that this resource is a Replicate database, and to use this value as the source database. This correlates to the identifier of another Amazon RDS Database to replicate

inputs.name: license_model
inputs.description: License model information for this DB instance. Optional, but required for some DB engines, i.e. Oracle SE1

inputs.name: replica_mode
inputs.description: Specifies whether the replica is in either mounted or open-read-only mode. This attribute is only supported by Oracle instances. Oracle replicas operate in open-read-only mode unless otherwise specified

inputs.name: iam_database_authentication_enabled
inputs.description: Specifies whether or not the mappings of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) accounts to database accounts are enabled

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: The ID of the Directory Service Active Directory domain to create the instance in

inputs.name: domain_iam_role_name
inputs.description: (Required if domain is provided) The name of the IAM role to be used when making API calls to the Directory Service

inputs.name: engine
inputs.description: The database engine to use

inputs.name: engine_version
inputs.description: The engine version to use

inputs.name: skip_final_snapshot
inputs.description: Determines whether a final DB snapshot is created before the DB instance is deleted. If true is specified, no DBSnapshot is created. If false is specified, a DB snapshot is created before the DB instance is deleted

inputs.name: snapshot_identifier
inputs.description: Specifies whether or not to create this database from a snapshot. This correlates to the snapshot ID you'd find in the RDS console, e.g: rds:production-2015-06-26-06-05

inputs.name: copy_tags_to_snapshot
inputs.description: On delete, copy all Instance tags to the final snapshot

inputs.name: final_snapshot_identifier_prefix
inputs.description: The name which is prefixed to the final snapshot on cluster destroy

inputs.name: instance_class
inputs.description: The instance type of the RDS instance

inputs.name: db_name
inputs.description: The DB name to create. If omitted, no database is created initially

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: Username for the master DB user

inputs.name: password
inputs.description:
Password for the master DB user. Note that this may show up in logs, and it will be stored in the state file.
  The password provided will not be used if `manage_master_user_password` is set to true.

inputs.name: manage_master_user_password
inputs.description: Set to true to allow RDS to manage the master user password in Secrets Manager

inputs.name: master_user_secret_kms_key_id
inputs.description:
The key ARN, key ID, alias ARN or alias name for the KMS key to encrypt the master user password secret in Secrets Manager.
  If not specified, the default KMS key for your Amazon Web Services account is used.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: The port on which the DB accepts connections

inputs.name: vpc_security_group_ids
inputs.description: List of VPC security groups to associate

inputs.name: availability_zone
inputs.description: The Availability Zone of the RDS instance

inputs.name: multi_az
inputs.description: Specifies if the RDS instance is multi-AZ

inputs.name: iops
inputs.description: The amount of provisioned IOPS. Setting this implies a storage_type of 'io1' or `gp3`. See `notes` for limitations regarding this variable for `gp3`

inputs.name: publicly_accessible
inputs.description: Bool to control if instance is publicly accessible

inputs.name: monitoring_interval
inputs.description: The interval, in seconds, between points when Enhanced Monitoring metrics are collected for the DB instance. To disable collecting Enhanced Monitoring metrics, specify 0. The default is 0. Valid Values: 0, 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, 60

inputs.name: monitoring_role_arn
inputs.description: The ARN for the IAM role that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs. Must be specified if monitoring_interval is non-zero

inputs.name: monitoring_role_name
inputs.description: Name of the IAM role which will be created when create_monitoring_role is enabled

inputs.name: monitoring_role_use_name_prefix
inputs.description: Determines whether to use `monitoring_role_name` as is or create a unique identifier beginning with `monitoring_role_name` as the specified prefix

inputs.name: monitoring_role_description
inputs.description: Description of the monitoring IAM role

inputs.name: create_monitoring_role
inputs.description: Create IAM role with a defined name that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs

inputs.name: monitoring_role_permissions_boundary
inputs.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the monitoring IAM role

inputs.name: allow_major_version_upgrade
inputs.description: Indicates that major version upgrades are allowed. Changing this parameter does not result in an outage and the change is asynchronously applied as soon as possible

inputs.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade
inputs.description: Indicates that minor engine upgrades will be applied automatically to the DB instance during the maintenance window

inputs.name: apply_immediately
inputs.description: Specifies whether any database modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window

inputs.name: maintenance_window
inputs.description: The window to perform maintenance in. Syntax: 'ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi'. Eg: 'Mon:00:00-Mon:03:00'

inputs.name: blue_green_update
inputs.description: Enables low-downtime updates using RDS Blue/Green deployments.

inputs.name: backup_retention_period
inputs.description: The days to retain backups for

inputs.name: backup_window
inputs.description: The daily time range (in UTC) during which automated backups are created if they are enabled. Example: '09:46-10:16'. Must not overlap with maintenance_window

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_assign_ipv6_address_on_creation
inputs.description: Specify true to indicate that network interfaces created in the specified subnet should be assigned an IPv6 address. Default is `false`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_enable_dns64
inputs.description: Indicates whether DNS queries made to the Amazon-provided DNS Resolver in this subnet should return synthetic IPv6 addresses for IPv4-only destinations. Default: `true`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_enable_resource_name_dns_aaaa_record_on_launch
inputs.description: Indicates whether to respond to DNS queries for instance hostnames with DNS AAAA records. Default: `true`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_enable_resource_name_dns_a_record_on_launch
inputs.description: Indicates whether to respond to DNS queries for instance hostnames with DNS A records. Default: `false`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_ipv6_prefixes
inputs.description: Assigns IPv6 redshift subnet id based on the Amazon provided /56 prefix base 10 integer (0-256). Must be of equal length to the corresponding IPv4 subnet list

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_ipv6_native
inputs.description: Indicates whether to create an IPv6-only subnet. Default: `false`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_private_dns_hostname_type_on_launch
inputs.description: The type of hostnames to assign to instances in the subnet at launch. For IPv6-only subnets, an instance DNS name must be based on the instance ID. For dual-stack and IPv4-only subnets, you can specify whether DNS names use the instance IPv4 address or the instance ID. Valid values: `ip-name`, `resource-name`

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_names
inputs.description: Explicit values to use in the Name tag on redshift subnets. If empty, Name tags are generated

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_suffix
inputs.description: Suffix to append to redshift subnets name

inputs.name: enable_public_redshift
inputs.description: Controls if redshift should have public routing table

inputs.name: create_redshift_subnet_route_table
inputs.description: Controls if separate route table for redshift should be created

inputs.name: redshift_route_table_tags
inputs.description: Additional tags for the redshift route tables

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_tags
inputs.description: Additional tags for the redshift subnets

inputs.name: create_redshift_subnet_group
inputs.description: Controls if redshift subnet group should be created

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_group_name
inputs.description: Name of redshift subnet group

inputs.name: redshift_subnet_group_tags
inputs.description: Additional tags for the redshift subnet group

inputs.name: redshift_dedicated_network_acl
inputs.description: Whether to use dedicated network ACL (not default) and custom rules for redshift subnets

inputs.name: redshift_inbound_acl_rules
inputs.description: Redshift subnets inbound network ACL rules

inputs.name: redshift_outbound_acl_rules
inputs.description: Redshift subnets outbound network ACL rules

inputs.description: Additional tags for the redshift subnets network ACL

inputs.description: Should be true if you want to provision NAT Gateways for each of your private networks

inputs.description: Used to pass a custom destination route for private NAT Gateway. If not specified, the default 0.0.0.0/0 is used as a destination route

inputs.description: Should be true if you want to provision a single shared NAT Gateway across all of your private networks

inputs.description: Should be true if you want only one NAT Gateway per availability zone. Requires `var.azs` to be set, and the number of `public_subnets` created to be greater than or equal to the number of availability zones specified in `var.azs`

inputs.name: enable_flow_log
inputs.description: Whether or not to enable VPC Flow Logs

inputs.name: vpc_flow_log_permissions_boundary
inputs.description: The ARN of the Permissions Boundary for the VPC Flow Log IAM Role

inputs.name: flow_log_max_aggregation_interval
inputs.description: The maximum interval of time during which a flow of packets is captured and aggregated into a flow log record. Valid Values: `60` seconds or `600` seconds

inputs.name: flow_log_traffic_type
inputs.description: The type of traffic to capture. Valid values: ACCEPT, REJECT, ALL

inputs.name: flow_log_destination_type
inputs.description: Type of flow log destination. Can be s3, kinesis-data-firehose or cloud-watch-logs

inputs.name: flow_log_log_format
inputs.description: The fields to include in the flow log record, in the order in which they should appear

inputs.name: flow_log_destination_arn
inputs.description: The ARN of the CloudWatch log group or S3 bucket where VPC Flow Logs will be pushed. If this ARN is a S3 bucket the appropriate permissions need to be set on that bucket's policy. When create_flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group is set to false this argument must be provided

inputs.name: flow_log_deliver_cross_account_role
inputs.description: (Optional) ARN of the IAM role that allows Amazon EC2 to publish flow logs across accounts.

inputs.name: flow_log_file_format
inputs.description: (Optional) The format for the flow log. Valid values: `plain-text`, `parquet`

inputs.name: flow_log_hive_compatible_partitions
inputs.description: (Optional) Indicates whether to use Hive-compatible prefixes for flow logs stored in Amazon S3

inputs.name: flow_log_per_hour_partition
inputs.description: (Optional) Indicates whether to partition the flow log per hour. This reduces the cost and response time for queries

inputs.name: vpc_flow_log_tags
inputs.description: Additional tags for the VPC Flow Logs

inputs.name: create_flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group
inputs.description: Whether to create CloudWatch log group for VPC Flow Logs

inputs.name: create_flow_log_cloudwatch_iam_role
inputs.description: Whether to create IAM role for VPC Flow Logs

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_iam_role_arn
inputs.description: The ARN for the IAM role that's used to post flow logs to a CloudWatch Logs log group. When flow_log_destination_arn is set to ARN of Cloudwatch Logs, this argument needs to be provided

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group_name_prefix
inputs.description: Specifies the name prefix of CloudWatch Log Group for VPC flow logs

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group_name_suffix
inputs.description: Specifies the name suffix of CloudWatch Log Group for VPC flow logs

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days
inputs.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the specified log group for VPC flow logs

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id
inputs.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data for VPC flow logs

inputs.name: flow_log_cloudwatch_log_group_skip_destroy
inputs.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
